content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to run a restaurant for NNN days (numbered 111 through NNN).

First, Chef needs to buy some plates. Each of these plates should have a positive integer capacity. Chef must decide in advance the number of plates MMM to buy as well as their capacities (not necessarily the same for each plate). A plate with capacity ccc cannot hold more than ccc fruits and it is called half-full if it holds at least ceil(c/2)ceil(c/2)ceil(c/2) fruits. Initially, all plates are empty and numbered 111 through MMM.

Chef needs to satisfy his guests on each of the NNN days. For each day iii:


On the morning of this day, Chef unlocks an infinite supply of fruit of the iii-th type. He may use these fruits any number of times during the iii-th day and all days that follow.
In the afternoon, he may choose up to 444 fruits and add them to some of the plates. The fruits may be of any types (possibly the same type) and they may be added to any plates independently from each other (possibly all to the same plate). For example, if this is the 555-th day and Chef has 333 plates, he may choose fruits of types {1,1,3,4}\{1, 1, 3, 4\}{1,1,3,4}, add fruits of types 111 and 444 to the second plate and fruits of types 111 and 333 to the third plate.
By the evening of this day, he must present a single plate containing at least one of each type of fruit between 111 and iii inclusive. Additionally, the plate must be at least half-full, otherwise it would look empty and his guests would not be satisfied.
Note that the same plate may be presented multiple times. When a plate is presented, the fruits on it remain (do not get eaten).

Help Chef decide the number of plates, the capacity of each plate, as well as the types of fruits to pick each day and the plates to add them to. Chef does not need to minimise the number of plates or their capacities, but the number of plates must not exceed 1,0001,0001,000 and their capacities must not exceed NNN. You may find any valid solution. It is guaranteed that under the given constraints, a solution always exists.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 100000
M must not exceed 1000 and each capacity must not exceed N
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line with an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output a constructive plan:
- First line: integer M – the number of plates (M ≤ 1000).
- Second line: M space‑separated integers C1 … CM, the capacities of the plates (1 ≤ Ci ≤ N).
- Then for each day i from 1 to N:
  * One line with integer Ki (1 ≤ Ki ≤ 4) – the number of fruits added that afternoon.
  * Ki lines each containing two integers F and P (1 ≤ F ≤ i, 1 ≤ P ≤ M) – the fruit type and the plate it is placed on.
The plan must satisfy the conditions described in the statement. Any valid plan is accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We use a single plate of capacity 3. Each day we add one fruit of the new type to this plate. After day i the plate holds i fruits, which is at least half of its capacity and contains all types 1..i.
With N=1 a single plate of capacity 1 suffices. On day 1 we add the only fruit type.

solution.approach:
A very simple construction works for every N.

* Use exactly one plate (M = 1) with capacity N. The capacity is enough for all N fruits and N ≤ capacity, so the plate never overflows.
* On day i add exactly one fruit of the newly unlocked type i to this plate. This uses Ki = 1 (which is ≤ 4) and places the fruit on plate 1.
* After day i the plate contains one fruit of each type 1…i, i.e., i fruits in total. Since i ≥ ceil(i/2) for all i ≥ 1, the plate is at least half‑full. The capacity N is ≥ i, so the plate never exceeds its limit.
* The same plate can be presented every day because fruits are never removed.

Thus the described plan is always valid, respects all limits, and is trivial to generate.
The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case, only printing the required lines.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (just a linear scan to output the plan). The total over all test cases is O(ΣN) ≤ 10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the output (only a few integer variables).
